title: What's Trade-in's STAR rate?
bodyHtml:
What’s the STAR rate?

The Satisfaction Ticket After Resolution (STAR) rate is a KPI that measures customer satisfaction after Trade-in interactions. Think of this rate as a way for your customers to show you some love!

 

How does it work?

Seven days after a Trade-in interaction, Back Market sends a survey to your customer, asking them to rate their experience with you in 3 specific categories: speed, courtesy, and relevance.

Here are some ways to guarantee a sky-high rate every time:

  • Speed: 
    • Process all orders within 2 business days of receiving them (see LPR action plan for more details)
    • Respond to all customer inquiries within 24 hours (see LRR action plan for more details)
  • Courtesy: 
    • Ensure your communication is objective, helpful, and kind
  • Relevance: 
    • Follow all processes and regulations found in the Back Market CGU and Quality Charter
    • Avoid making unnecessary counteroffers (see CO Rate action plan for more details)
    • Confirm necessary counteroffers are fair to customers to avoid rejected offers (see Cancellation Rate action plan for more details) 

Your seller’s STAR rate is then calculated by dividing the number of positive survey responses by the total number of survey responses. This rate can be found on your dashboard and reflects the last 30 days of Trade-in interactions.

 

How can I improve my STAR rate?

You can boost your STAR rate by providing consistent, high-quality service. This means your customers will be more likely to leave positive reviews and return for additional devices in the future.

If you have any questions, get in touch with your Back Market representative.
